name: Hawaiian Teriyaki Roast Pork
recipeInstructions:
1. Pierce the meaty parts of the pork with a fork.
2. Place the pork in a large plastic bag.
3. Combine the remaining 5 ingredients (Kikkoman Teriyaki Sauce, brown sugar, dry sherry, minced ginger, and garlic) in a bowl.
4. Pour the mixture over the pork in the plastic bag.
5. Press any air out of the bag and securely close it.
6. Refrigerate for 8 hours or overnight, turning the bag occasionally.
7. Remove the pork from the bag, fat side down, into an 8-inch square microwave-safe baking dish.
8. Brush the pork thoroughly with the reserved marinade.
9. Cover the pork loosely with wax paper.
10. Microwave on Medium-high (70%) for 30 minutes, rotating the dish once and brushing with the reserved marinade.
11. Turn the pork over and cover it again.
12. Microwave on Medium-high for another 30 minutes, or until a meat thermometer inserted in the thickest part registers 165°F, rotating the dish once and brushing with the marinade.
13. Remove the pork from the microwave and let it stand for 10 minutes before slicing.
